focus_areas: morsel-driven parallel scan of 60M lineitem with 64-core distribution
SIMD-vectorized l_shipdate filtering (high selectivity 0.987 requires all rows)
thread-local hash aggregation into 6 groups (extremely low cardinality)
dictionary decoding for l_returnflag and l_linestatus (already encoded)
utilize l_shipdate_sorted index for zone map pruning on date range
strategy_notes: Q1 is already competitive but can be pushed further. Implement morsel-based scanning with 64 threads (target ~940K rows/morsel with L3 44MB cache). Use SIMD for date comparisons and arithmetic on quantity/price/tax. Each thread maintains thread-local aggregation hash table (6 groups) to avoid lock contention. Final merge aggregates results. Expected: maintain or improve current 48ms performance while demonstrating sustained parallelism.
reasoning: GenDB shows strong performance on Q1 (1.8-3.2x faster than DuckDB) but critical underperformance on Q3 (3.4-5x slower) and Q6 (1.4-1.5x slower). The 64-core, 376GB hardware enables aggressive parallelism. Priority: (1) fix Q3 join strategy and selectivity pushdown, (2) optimize Q6 predicate evaluation order, (3) maintain Q1's strong scaling through morsel-driven parallelism and SIMD.
